description: Task requires interaction with external parties who have no obligation or incentive to respond, creating unverifiable completion criteria.

anonymized_example.title: Get confirmation from [External Project] team on integration timeline
anonymized_example.description: Reach out to the core team of [External Project] and get written confirmation of their planned integration timeline with our network.
anonymized_example.failure_mode: Contributor has no leverage over external party. Task completion depends entirely on whether a stranger replies to a cold outreach. Contributor cannot succeed through effort alone.

classifier_signal: Completion depends on response from party outside the network with no established relationship or incentive to engage.
guard_message: Task completion depends on uncontrollable external response. Reframe as: produce the outreach artifact (draft, channels identified, value prop) rather than requiring the response itself.

description: Unrelated work wrapped in network terminology to appear on-mission.

anonymized_example.title: Build [Unrelated Industry] workflow optimizer as PFT-powered service
anonymized_example.description: Create a [specific vertical] tool that uses PFT tokens for payment and task coordination, bringing [vertical] professionals into the Post Fiat ecosystem.
anonymized_example.failure_mode: The actual work (building a vertical SaaS tool) has nothing to do with network infrastructure, intelligence, or coordination. 'Uses PFT for payment' is a veneer — any token could substitute.

classifier_signal: Remove all PFT/network references from the task. If the remaining work has zero overlap with network mission (AI coordination, financial intelligence, contributor tooling, network growth), it's off-grid regardless of payment-rail framing.
guard_message: Task is an unrelated vertical service using PFT as payment rail. Payment-rail usage alone does not make arbitrary work on-mission. The work must advance network infrastructure, intelligence, or coordination.

description: Task describes functionality that already exists in production, either in core infrastructure or community tooling.

anonymized_example.title: Implement [Feature X] for the network
anonymized_example.description: Build a system that [does exactly what an existing shipped tool does], with [properties that match the existing implementation's spec].
anonymized_example.failure_mode: Contributor spends days building something that already exists. If they discover it mid-task, they've wasted time. If they don't discover it, the network pays for duplicate infrastructure.

classifier_signal: Task description matches the feature set of existing shipped tooling. Check: (1) core validator codebase, (2) community projects registry, (3) ecosystem repos (agtico/*, postfiatorg/*).
guard_message: This functionality appears to already exist in [specific location]. If the intent is to improve or extend it, reframe the task to reference the existing implementation and specify what's missing.

description: Task requires verifying claims against data sources that are inaccessible, non-existent, or contradicted by available evidence.

anonymized_example.title: Verify [Metric] from [Source] and report findings
anonymized_example.description: Pull [specific metric] from [named source] and cross-reference against [second source] to confirm accuracy of network claims.
anonymized_example.failure_mode: The named source doesn't expose the claimed metric, or the metric definition has changed, or the source requires authentication that contributors don't have. Task is structurally uncompletable as specified.

classifier_signal: Task references specific external data sources. Verify: (1) source is publicly accessible, (2) claimed metric/endpoint actually exists, (3) contributor can access without special credentials.
guard_message: Verification target is inaccessible or non-existent. Before issuing: confirm the data source exposes the claimed metric publicly. If authentication required, provide it or reframe task.
